{"key_event":"Postwar demobilization, radical unrest, and repression","reasoning":"Civil society gained from industrial urban growth, radical print, petitioning, reform meetings, and successful pressure against the income tax, though repression of Luddites, Spa Fields, and Pentrich limited the advance. State power remained strong through wartime mobilization and coercive laws, but by 1818 demobilization, tax repeal, spending cuts, and postwar instability left it slightly below the prior wartime level.","society_change":0.3,"society_power":6.4,"state_change":-0.2,"state_power":7.5}
